_description: Salary scale definitions for Sierra Leone Colonial Office List extractions. Scale letters (A-F) were used from ~1924-1940. Min/max represent the widest possible range across all appointment cohorts (pre/post Oct 1923, post Aug 1935). Source: RAG narrative sections from 1929, 1939, 1940 editions.
_note: Post-1948 scales (M.3, N.1, N.2, C.1, C.2, C.3) use a different system and are NOT resolved here.

A.description: Scale A: 400-450 starting, rising by 30l. to 720l., by 40l. to 920l., with seniority allowance of 72l. from 720l.

B.description: Scale B: 400-500 starting, rising by 30l. to 720l., by 40l. to 960l., with seniority allowance of 72l. from 720l. (Post-1935 cohort can reach 1000l.)

C.description: Scale C: 450-490 starting, rising by 30l. to 720l., by 40l. to 920l., with seniority allowance of 72l. from 720l.

D.description: Scale D: 360-400 starting, rising by 12l. to 460l., the last increment being 18l.

E.description: Scale E: 390-420 starting, rising by 12l. to 460l., the last increment being 18l.

F.description: Scale F: 400-440 starting, rising by 12l. to 500l., the last increment being 18l.

applicable_years.note: Scales A-F defined in narrative sections of these editions. Years 1949-1951 use 'A'/'B' labels but with different (post-war) definitions that are not resolved here.
